description: structural conformance lint for a spec doc — exists, has a title, carries normative statements, covers required topics (P0-V10)
rules: read-only — reads the spec, writes only the lint report
a normative statement is an RFC-2119 keyword (MUST / MUST NOT / SHALL / SHALL NOT / REQUIRED)
REQUIRE pins topic coverage; MIN_NORMATIVE pins that the spec actually decides something
usage: Set SPEC (path to a .md). Optional MIN_NORMATIVE (default 1), REQUIRE (';'-separated substrings each of which must appear). Output: doclint.json.
limitation: Structural lint only — it checks the spec exists and is normatively non-empty and on-topic, not that each MUST maps to a criterion ID (the full truth-in-labeling cross-ref) nor cross-language conformance (the vectors perk, which needs infra/cwp).
summary: structural conformance lint for a spec doc — exists, title, normative, on-topic (P0-V10)
